body_txt: Overweight people can be very self-conscious. It's understandable because we tend to associate many negative traits and behaviors with obesity . Thus, insecure overweight people may come up with any excuse they can to avoid the stigma of being labeled as such . It isn't that they are fat, they are just big-boned. Or maybe it's just baby fat. Maybe they have a gland disorder, a slow metabolism, or they're retaining water, or they're bloated from an allergic reaction . If they're a child, maybe it's a growth spurt. It has nothing to do with lack of exercise or the triple cheeseburger, bacon chili fries, and chocolate-covered strawberries that they just ate . They might also insist on being called something like "chubby" or "plump", despite them meaning the same thing as fat . In Real Life , many of these excuses are accurate in at least some cases, such as hypothyroidism. A form of Insistent Terminology . See also Does This Make Me Look Fat? , Hollywood Pudgy , and Fat and Proud . Not to be confused with another kind of bone . Contrast Fluffy, Not Fat for when they really aren't fat; they just look that way due to fur or feathers.
